The '''68th British Academy Film Awards''', more commonly known as the [[BAFTAs]], were held on 8 February 2015 at the [[Royal Opera House]] in [[London]], honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2014 in film|2014]]. Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2014.<ref name="FAI">{{cite web|url=http://www.bafta.org/film/awards/film-awards-information|title=Film Awards Information|publisher=British Academy of Film and Television Arts|access-date=8 February 2015}}</ref>

The nominations were announced on 9 January 2015 by [[Stephen Fry]] and actor [[Sam Claflin]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Entered Films List 2014/15 |url=https://static.bafta.org/uploads_pre_202411/entered-films-201415.pdf |website=bafta.org |publisher=British Academy of Film and Television Arts |access-date=26 January 2026 |ref=Entered}}</ref><ref name="nominees">{{cite web |url=http://awards.bafta.org/award/2015/film |title=Film in 2015 |date=9 January 2015 |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] |access-date=9 January 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/av/entertainment-arts-30740506|title=Baftas 2015: Fry and Claflin announce nominations|date=9 January 2015|work=[[BBC News]]|access-date=8 February 2015}}</ref> The ceremony was broadcast on [[BBC One]] and [[BBC Three]].<ref name="FAI"/> It was hosted by Stephen Fry for the tenth time in the award's history.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.bafta.org/press/press-releases/stephen-fry-returns-to-host-ee-british-academy-film-awards |title=Stephen Fry Returns to Host EE British Academy Film Awards |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] |date=11 December 2014 |access-date=11 December 2014}}</ref> The ceremony opened with a number "[[Stevie (Kasabian song)|Stevie]]" by the British rock band [[Kasabian]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/music/news/baftas-poach-kasabian-to-open-show-after-brit-awards-snub-rockers-10009133.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150128234247/http://www.independent.co.uk/arts-entertainment/music/news/baftas-poach-kasabian-to-open-show-after-brit-awards-snub-rockers-10009133.html |archive-date=2015-01-28 |url-access=limited |url-status=live|title=Kasabian poached by Baftas after Brits snub|last=Sherwin|first=Adam|date=28 January 2015|work=[[The Independent]]|access-date=8 February 2015}}</ref>

''[[Boyhood (2014 film)|Boyhood]]'' won three of its five nominations, including [[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]], [[BAFTA Award for Best Direction|Best Director]] for [[Richard Linklater]], and [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Supporting Role|Best Actress in a Supporting Role]] for [[Patricia Arquette]].<ref name="Brown">{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2015/feb/08/baftas-2015-boyhood-wins-top-honours-but-grand-budapest-hotel-checks-out-with-most|title=Baftas 2015: Boyhood wins top honours but Grand Budapest Hotel checks out with most|last=Brown|first=Mark|date=8 February 2015|work=[[The Guardian]]|access-date=8 February 2015}}</ref><ref name="Winners">{{cite news |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/entertainment-arts-30741418 |title=Bafta Film Awards 2015: Winners |access-date=8 February 2015 |work=BBC News|date=9 January 2015 }}</ref> [[Eddie Redmayne]] won [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Leading Role|Best Actor in a Leading Role]] for ''[[The Theory of Everything (2014 film)|The Theory of Everything]]'', [[Julianne Moore]] won [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Leading Role|Best Actress in a Leading Role]] for ''[[Still Alice]]'', and [[J. K. Simmons]] won [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Supporting Role|Best Actor in a Supporting Role]] for ''[[Whiplash (2014 film)|Whiplash]]''.<ref name="Brown"/> ''[[The Grand Budapest Hotel]]'' won five of its eleven nominations, the most of any film. [[Jack O'Connell (actor)|Jack O'Connell]] won the [[BAFTA Rising Star Award|Rising Star Award]].<ref name="Brown"/><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/culture/film/baftas/11396683/Bafta-2015-film-awards-live.html|title=Bafta 2015 film awards - as it happened|date=8 February 2015|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=12 February 2015}}</ref> ''The Theory of Everything'', directed by [[James Marsh (director)|James Marsh]], was voted [[BAFTA Award for Best British Film|Outstanding British Film]] of 2014, while ''[[The Lego Movie]]'' won [[BAFTA Award for Best Animated Film|Best Animated Film]] and ''[[Citizenfour]]'' won [[BAFTA Award for Best Documentary|Best Documentary]].<ref name="Brown"/>

The telecast garnered more than 5.09 million viewers in UK, with the viewing figures slightly higher than [[67th British Academy Film Awards|previous year ceremony]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.barb.co.uk/whats-new/weekly-top-30?|title=Weekly Top 30 Programmes|publisher=[[Broadcasters' Audience Research Board|BARB]]|access-date=3 May 2015}}</ref>
